summary: Linked several item units by flipping item objects to Matching, adding exact item state tables/data symbols, and tightening signatures/codegen in Lipstick Spore, Pikachu Thunder Jolt Air, Likelike, and partially Hitodeman. The PR title names itpikachutjoltair, itlipstickspore, and itlikelike; the diff also changes ithitodeman and configure.py marks it Matching, but the author noted Hitodeman was still difficult and might need resplitting. decomp.dev reported +2360 matched code bytes, +15984 linked code bytes, +160 matched data bytes, +688 linked data bytes, and 9 new matches.

key_files.role: Changed itlipstickspore.c, itpikachutjoltair.c, ithitodeman.c, and itlikelike.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted local/global scopes, local @NNN data labels, state table/data symbols, and named zero_vec.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F6680 and corrected it_8029A114 argument order/types.
Updated caller of it_8029A114 to pass facing_dir before arg4.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F71D8 and solved small codegen/type issues around spawn/update/collision callbacks.
Matched Dropped, UnkMotion6_Phys, and it_802DC4BC using inlined/duplicated ECB transition logic and stack padding.
Added state tables it_803F8128 and it_803F8180 plus codegen helpers, improving but not fully solving Hitodeman data per bot report.
Replaced ftCo_800C78B0 UNK_PARAMS with HSD_GObj* gobj and HSD_GObj* owner.
Kept the ftCo_800C78B0 prototype consistent with the source.

what_changed: configure.py now treats four item source files as Matching: itlipstickspore.c, itpikachutjoltair.c, ithitodeman.c, and itlikelike.c.

symbols.txt changes many anonymous constants/strings/floats from global it_80xxxxxx names to local @NNN labels, marks small Coll functions local, adds a local ...data.0 label, and renames it_803B86D8 to global zero_vec with Vec3-sized 0xC data.
itlipstickspore.c gained an explicit ItemStateTable it_803F6680 for UnkMotion0/1 and changed it_8029A114 from (gobj, pos, int, f32) to (gobj, pos, f32 facing_dir, s32 arg4).
itlipstick.c was updated to call it_8029A114(ip->owner, arg1, arg2, arg3), matching the corrected facing_dir/arg4 order.

itpikachutjoltair.c gained ItemStateTable it_803F71D8, moved/implemented itPikachutjoltair_UnkMotion0_Coll as a bool returning false, used GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ, cleaned NULL/zero initialization, and removed unnecessary casts in cleanup callbacks.

itlikelike.c added static inline it_802DC4BC_ptr, expanded the Dropped and UnkMotion6_Phys transition paths inline, adjusted itECB locals and PAD_STACK values, and kept Vec3 sp50 initialized from zero_vec in it_802DC4BC.

ithitodeman.c removed placeholder-style includes, added ItemStateTable arrays it_803F8128 and it_803F8180, added an extern inline sqrtf implementation, added an Article accessor inline, typed it_802D43AC(Item_GObj*), and adjusted stack padding/literals.
ft_0C31.c/h replaced ftCo_800C78B0(UNK_PARAMS) with ftCo_800C78B0(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*) to give item/fighter capture code a concrete owner-style signature.
smart_moves: The linkage fix was not just configure.py: the PR made the item .data real by defining ItemStateTable arrays in C and aligning symbol scopes with what the compiler emits.
Correcting it_8029A114's parameter order and updating itlipstick.c fixed a near-match that was sensitive to argument register assignment.

The Likelike changes intentionally duplicated/inlined transition logic, including a comment labeled permuterslop, showing a pragmatic codegen-first approach when a cleaner helper call produced wrong registers or stack layout.
Using typed callbacks such as it_802D43AC(Item_GObj*) removed casts and made callback signatures agree with item collision helpers.
Renaming shared zero vector data to zero_vec and demoting compiler-local constants to @NNN avoided over-exported symbols while preserving exact data layout.
The diff used small PAD_STACK changes and scoped locals instead of large rewrites, minimizing behavioral risk while solving one-byte/near-match differences.
decomp_lessons: Item object linkage often depends on matching state table data before code can be safely marked Matching; define ItemStateTable arrays with exact motion IDs and Anim/Phys/Coll function pointers.
Small local/static scope decisions matter: table-only Coll functions should be local/static when symbols indicate local scope.
Do not leave UNK_PARAMS or wrong argument order in shared headers when caller codegen depends on concrete HSD_GObj*, owner, facing_dir, or s32 argument placement.
Compiler-generated literals and strings should usually be @NNN local labels in symbols.txt, not global it_80xxxxxx names, unless there is evidence of a named/shared object.
Matching may require source shaping that looks redundant: inline wrappers, duplicated blocks, explicit PAD_STACK, and carefully scoped locals can be legitimate tactics.
The author's Hitodeman note and bot's 63.37% .data result suggest that stubborn item data may need resplitting or further data-boundary investigation rather than more local code churn.
naming_conventions: Item files use lowercase kind names such as itpikachutjoltair, itlipstickspore, itlikelike, and ithitodeman.
Unknown item state-table data kept address-based names: it_803F6680, it_803F71D8, it_803F8128, and it_803F8180.
Motion callbacks retain the pattern itKind_UnkMotionN_Anim, itKind_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itKind_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Item logic callbacks retain it_2725_LogicNN_Event names when semantic names are not known, such as it_2725_Logic5_Dropped and it_2725_Logic107_Absorbed.
Compiler-local constants in symbols.txt use @NNN labels and local scope; shared/global vector data can receive a semantic name such as zero_vec.
Improved parameter names in this PR include owner, facing_dir, arg4, gobj, ip, and ecb.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with precise sizes including 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, and 28 bytes to steer stack-frame layout.
Added extern inline sqrtf using __frsqrte refinement in ithitodeman.c, indicating inline math expansion was needed for matching.
Introduced small inline helpers it_802D472C_inline and it_802DC4BC_ptr to influence inlining/register allocation.
Duplicated Likelike ECB reset/transition code in Dropped and UnkMotion6_Phys instead of relying only on it_802DAA10.
Changed unnecessary casts and literals, for example removing (f32)/(f64) around M_PI_2 * ip->facing_dir and using 0.0f consistently.
Changed function and data symbol scope in symbols.txt from global to local where the emitted object expected static/local symbols.
Moved/defined itPikachutjoltair_UnkMotion0_Coll as an actual bool function returning false while marking its symbol local.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the provided slice.
PR body from lukechampine: Hitodeman was attempted but not fully figured out and might need to be resplit.

decomp-dev bot reported 9 new matches, including itlipstickspore .data, itpikachutjoltair .data, itlikelike .rodata, it_8029A114, it_2725_Logic107_Reflected, it_2725_Logic24_Spawned, it_2725_Logic5_Dropped, itLikelike_UnkMotion6_Phys, and it_802DC4BC.
Bot also reported ithitodeman .data improved from 0.00% to 63.37%, supporting the author's note that Hitodeman data was not fully solved in the evidence shown.
follow_up_queries: Investigate why ithitodeman .data remained 63.37% and whether the file needs to be resplit.
Audit later PRs for semantic names replacing it_803F8128, it_803F8180, it_803F6680, or it_803F71D8.
Search for other callers of it_8029A114 to ensure the facing_dir and arg4 order stayed consistent.
Compare Likelike ECB transition helpers to see whether it_802DC4BC_ptr or the duplicated permuterslop block was later cleaned up.
Look for subsequent Hitodeman work around ordering_func, inline sqrtf, and state-table/data boundary fixes.
